US officials say the real cost of the Iran war is about $40–50 billion, nearly double the Pentagon’s public estimate of $25 billion. The Pentagon’s figure excludes some costs, including damaged or destroyed equipment and military installations. A major portion of the cost comes from replacing used munitions, including expensive systems like MQ-9 Reaper drones. Additional hidden expenses include fuel, deployments, base operations, and other military logistics. The war is also contributing to higher fuel and food prices for US consumers, with some estimates suggesting about $150 extra per household monthly. Israel Palestine Gaza Iran

today Went to see my beloved GP of 20 years. He took my blood for the first time for us. Had the best trick. You know the strap they put on your arm above where they're taking the blood from? After they take the needle out and put the cotton wad/tape on, get them to put the strap with the same pressure *over* the wad while they finish up their paperwork. It means that tape/wad is going to be super secure, you're not holding it, bleeding will be over faster too. Genius.

LetterboxdFriday In 1946, in a 'Where were we before we were so rudely interrupted?'' gesture, 11 films tied for the top prize at the Cannes Film Festival. These are 4 of those films. ANTOINE & ANTOINETTE - 🇫🇷 A working class couple win the lottery and lose the ticket. Masterfully directed. Comfortably the best film of these four ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ PASTORAL SYMPHONY - 🇫🇷 A pastor takes in a blind orphan, falls in love with her when she grows up... and his love rival is his own son. So... ick. My favourite moment is when it suddenly occurs to them (after she's a fully grown adult) that a surgeon might be able to fix her eyesight. And he does. All kinds of stupid, but entertaining ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ MARIA CANDELARIA - 🇲🇽 The intentional artificiality is nice -- almost Powell & Pressburger level -- but it's VERY melodramatic even for the 40s ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ MEN WITHOUT WINGS - 🇨🇿 🇸🇰 [Actually Czechoslovakia] Solid Czech Resistance versus Nazis thriller ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ LastFourWatched

A common delusion among tech-savvy people is that we will continue to be tech-savvy even when we are very old. We won't. We _might_ not be completely helpless in a few specific fields related to our core interests, but we will _not_ be able to keep up with the latest advances, user interfaces, IT security measures, and so forth. So if you don't have any kids of your own who are willing to assist you, make sure you stay friends a couple of young people who might be willing to help you out, mkay? ParentalTechStupport

"Global production of conventional crude oil—the easy stuff, the stuff that bursts out of the ground under its own pressure peaked around 2005. DreamLog A friend and I were visiting one of our old school premises since there was an event happening in the assembly hall there, but we decided to bail on it half way through because it was too busy. After we left the hallway the dream turned into a somewhat nerve-wracking horror sequence where we had to avoid everyone and find a safe space to hide and rest. We did find refuge in an old dark dusty stairwell until someone set the fire alarm off and everyone started pouring through it.

Today: Il "primo maggio" non è solo una festa Il 1° maggio è un giorno di festa perché ricorda una lunga battaglia per la dignità del lavoro. C’era un tempo in cui la vita non apparteneva davvero a chi lavorava. Si entrava in fabbrica all’alba e se ne usciva a notte fonda, dopo turni massacranti che potevano arrivare anche a 14 o 16 ore... May 1st is not just a holiday. May 1st is a holiday because it commemorates a long struggle for the dignity of labor. There was a time when life didn’t truly belong to those who worked. You would enter the factory at dawn and leave at nightfall, after grueling shifts that could reach 14 or 16 hours… today.it/attualita/primo-maggi o-perche-festa.html

Il Fatto Quotidiano: Oroscopo di maggio: “Toro, il 16 ci sarà un nuovo inizio personale. Acquario, arrivano cambiamenti improvvisi ma costruttivi. Pesci? Serve chiarezza” Maggio si apre con un novilunio in Scorpione che porta a galla emozioni profonde e processi di trasformazione già in atto. È un mese che alterna bisogno di stabilità e desiderio di cambiamento, con due momenti lunari importanti: la Luna Nuova del 16 in Toro, che richiama concretezza e radicamento, e la Luna Piena del 31 in Sagittario, che apre prospettive più ampie e invita a guardare oltre i limiti attuali. Mercurio entra in Toro il 3, favorendo riflessioni pratiche e decisioni più lente ma solide, per poi passare in Gemelli il 17, accelerando comunicazioni e scambi. Il 21 il Sole entra in Gemelli, portando leggerezza, movimento e nuove connessioni. Venere il 19 si sposta in Cancro, accentuando il bisogno di protezione emotiva e sincerità nei rapporti, mentre Marte nello stesso giorno passa in Toro, rendendo l’azione più concreta e determinata. Urano in Gemelli spinge verso cambiamenti improvvisi e innovazioni nel modo di pensare e comunicare. L'articolo Oroscopo di maggio: “Toro, il 16 ci sarà un nuovo inizio personale. Acquario, arrivano cambiamenti improvvisi ma costruttivi. Pesci? Serve chiarezza” proviene da Il Fatto Quotidiano. May Horoscope: “Taurus, the 16th will be a new personal beginning. Aquarius, sudden but constructive changes are arriving. Pisces? Clarity is needed.” May opens with a new moon in Scorpio, bringing to the surface deep emotions and transformation processes already in progress. It’s a month that alternates between a need for stability and a desire for change, with two important lunar events: the New Moon on the 16th in Taurus, which calls for concreteness and grounding, and the Full Moon on the 31st in Sagittarius, which opens up broader perspectives and invites us to look beyond current limitations. Mercury enters Taurus on the 3rd, favoring practical reflections and more deliberate but solid decisions, before moving to Gemini on the 17th, accelerating communications and exchanges. On the 21st, the Sun enters Gemini, bringing lightness, movement, and new connections. Venus moves to Cancer on the 19th, intensifying the need for emotional protection and sincerity in relationships, while Mars passes to Taurus on the same day, making action more concrete and determined. Uranus in Gemini pushes towards sudden changes and innovations in thinking and communication. The horoscope article “Taurus, on the 16th there will be a new personal beginning. Aquarius, sudden but constructive changes are arriving. Pisces? Clarity is needed” comes from Il Fatto Quotidiano.
